title: State-Based Validation
concept: Verifying task success by checking final environment states (like database records or file updates) directly.

exercises.prompt: What does an LLM do when judging task success?

options.text: It checks final results like database records.
It reads execution logs and gives scores.
It runs the task again to compare outcomes.

exercises.explain: An LLM judges task success by reading execution logs and scoring task completeness.

cards.title: Understanding State-Based Validation
cards.body: State-Based Validation means checking the final state of the environment to see if a task succeeded. This can involve looking at updated records or files.

cards.title: What is State-Based Validation?
cards.body: State-Based Validation is the process of verifying the success of a task by directly inspecting the final results or states of the system, like database updates.

cards.title: Think of It Like a Checklist
cards.body: Imagine you check off items on a checklist after completing a task. State-Based Validation is like checking the completed items to confirm everything was done correctly.

cards.title: Example of State-Based Validation
cards.body: If an AI updates a database to add a new user, State-Based Validation would involve checking the database to ensure that the new user is indeed listed there.

cards.title: Myth: State-Based Validation Is Unreliable
cards.body: It's a common myth that checking results directly isn't trustworthy. In fact, looking at final states provides clear evidence of success.

cards.title: Before and After State Check
cards.body: In State-Based Validation, we compare what was there before and after the task to see if it succeeded.

left.text: Old database record without new user

right.text: Updated database record with new user

exercises.prompt: What does State-Based Validation check to confirm task success?

options.text: The scoring given by the LLM.
The final state of the environment.
The time taken to complete the task.
The logs of conversation while executing.

exercises.explain: State-Based Validation checks the final state of the environment to confirm if the task was successful.

exercises.prompt: State-Based Validation only involves reading execution logs. True or False?
exercises.explain: False, State-Based Validation focuses on checking the final state of the environment, not just reading logs.

exercises.prompt: To verify task success, State-Based Validation checks the final ______ of the environment.
exercises.sentence: To verify task success, State-Based Validation checks the final state of the environment.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'state'. State-Based Validation examines the final state to confirm success.

exercises.prompt: Which method directly checks the success of a task by looking at the end results?
exercises.explain: State-Based Validation directly checks the success of a task by looking at the end results or states.

exercises.prompt: In which scenario would you best use State-Based Validation?

options.text: When judging the quality of a conversation.
When needing to confirm a user was added to a database.
When assessing how fast a task was completed.
When reviewing the log files after a task.

exercises.explain: You should use State-Based Validation to confirm a user was added to a database because this directly checks the final state of the environment, ensuring the task was successful.
